name: Shoot For Par
about: Shoot For Par is a specialized golf instruction and simulation facility in Oregon, Missouri, focusing on a holistic approach to golf improvement through mental, physical, and strategic training. The venue operates on a foundation of evidence-based methods, combining elite golf instruction with TPI fitness assessment, military discipline, and mental coaching. It caters to dedicated golfers who are committed to comprehensive self-improvement, starting with a mandatory physical assessment and a 90-day fitness plan as the foundation for all swing instruction. The facility offers personalized coaching, club fitting services, and a library of doctrinal training materials designed to build self-sufficient golfers.
why_golfers_like_it: Comprehensive, data-driven approach starting with physical assessment
Elite credentials including TPI, PGTAA, and specialized golf fitness certifications
Structured training system focused on self-sufficiency and long-term results
Access to advanced technology like Foresight GCQUAD for fitting and instruction

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, you should bring your own golf clubs for instruction and practice sessions. The facility also offers comprehensive club fitting services with 60 head/shaft options for irons and KRANK GOLF driver fittings.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The website does not mention any food or drink services. It is advisable to contact the venue directly to confirm their policy on outside food and beverages.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: This information is not specified on the website. As a training and instruction studio rather than a public play venue, the focus is on skill development rather than simulated rounds of golf.

faq.question: Is this venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, Shoot For Par appears to be kid-friendly. The description mentions building 'golfers' without age restriction, and the author, David Frederick, has credentials and materials suitable for junior development, including Certified Putting Instructor status.
